content:
Press enter or click to view image in full size

2 min read
Just now

--

Think about it.
When you tell an architect you want a house, they don't give you an empty piece of land and wish you luck.
When you tell a chef you want pasta, they don't hand you flour, tomatoes, and cheese.
They make the first version.
So why do design tools still start with a blank page?
A blinking cursor.
An empty canvas.
We've accepted it for years.
But it's actually a terrible user experience.
People don't start with empty screens.
They start with ideas.
I want a fitness app.
I want a landing page.
I want an admin dashboard.
I want my website to feel like Apple.
Those are the real starting points.
The blank screen is a limitation of the software, not of the human.
That thought kept bothering me.
Instead of asking how we could make design tools better, I started asking why there was a blank screen in the first place.
That single question became the beginning of DiddyDesign.
Instead of drawing every box yourself, you describe your idea.
The AI creates the first version.
You edit.
You refine.
You make it yours.
The goal isn't to replace designers.
It's to remove the most frustrating part of creating.
Starting.
I genuinely believe that, in a few years, opening a design tool and seeing a blank canvas will feel as outdated as dial-up internet.
We'll wonder why we ever thought that was normal.
Maybe I'm wrong.
But if I'm right...
This is only the beginning.
What do you think AI will make feel outdated next?
I'd love to hear your thoughts.
DiddyDesign → https://diddydesign.com
#ArtificialIntelligence #AI #Design #ProductDesign #UX #Startups #SaaS #BuildInPublic #Innovation #Technology
